items.title: What is hantavirus? Everything you need to know
items.summary_auto: A hantavirus outbreak aboard a luxury cruise ship is suspected in the deaths of three people and causing sickness in others. Here's what you need to know about the disease and how common it is in Canada.

items.title: Carney to name Canada's next governor general on Tuesday: sources
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney is set to announce on Tuesday who he’s appointing to become Canada’s next governor general, CBC News has learned.

items.title: May the 4th (be with you): Why Star Wars has stuck with fans
items.summary_auto: Star Wars Day — May 4 — is a pseudo holiday for fans like Kel Spoering, especially when she may be able to thank Star Wars for her existence. "May the 4th" has been calendared as Star Wars Day because of its punny reference to the science fiction franchise's iconic phrase, "May the Force be with you."

items.title: Toronto Maple Leafs introduce Chayka, Sundin in new management roles
items.summary_auto: The Maple Leafs introduced franchise legend Mats Sundin as its new senior executive adviser of hockey operations and John Chayka as general manager on Monday.

items.title: Raptors reflect on 'solid' season and return to playoffs after first-round loss
items.summary_auto: Toronto Raptors all-star forward Scottie Barnes stopped short of calling the NBA team's campaign a success at an end-of-season availability Monday, saying the players learned a lot about themselves and had a "solid" season.

items.title: Calgary father accused of murdering children, driving bodies to police station, makes first court appearance
items.summary_auto: A Calgary father accused of murdering his young children before driving their bodies to a police station where officers say he called 911 and confessed, made a brief court appearance today and will remain in custody for the foreseeable future.

items.title: Government's strategy to protect critical infrastructure from climate change falling short: report
items.summary_auto: The federal government's strategy to protect more than $100 billion worth of key infrastructure from the destructive impacts of climate change has significant gaps, with three key departments well behind where they need to be.

items.title: Britney Spears avoids jail time for DUI after pleading guilty to lesser charge
items.summary_auto: Britney Spears pleaded guilty through her lawyer Monday to a lesser charge that will allow her to avoid jail time after prosecutors accused her of driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs in Southern California.

items.title: Copper theft attempt causing Telus service outage across northwest B.C.
items.summary_auto: Telus is estimating its internet, TV and phone services will be restored to northwest B.C. by late Monday afternoon after vandals attempted to steal some of the company's copper cables.

items.title: Washouts, flooding close roads in areas across Saskatchewan
items.summary_auto: The Saskatchewan Public Safety Agency said there are 15 active local emergencies in the province, with the possibility of more on the way as dozens of communities grapple with a torrent of spring flooding.

items.title: Dozens of Sask. communities grapple with torrent of spring flooding
items.summary_auto: The Saskatchewan Public Safety Agency said there are 15 active local emergencies in the province, with the possibility of more on the way as dozens of communities grapple with a torrent of spring flooding.

items.title: Evacuations planned as suspected hantavirus outbreak traps 150, including 4 Canadians, on cruise ship
items.summary_auto: Medics were working on Monday to evacuate two people with symptoms of the deadly hantavirus ‌after a suspected outbreak on a luxury cruise ship held off West Africa that was carrying mostly British, American and Spanish passengers, along with four Canadians, officials said.

items.title: RCMP say U.S. tourist died in accident in Banff National Park
items.summary_auto: RCMP say a 25-year-old American man has died in Johnston Canyon in Banff National Park. Staff Sgt. Mike Buxton-Carr, Banff's RCMP detachment commander, says the incident is being considered as an accidental death during recreational activity.

items.title: The moment a Nova Scotia hockey fan fell onto the ice
items.summary_auto: An 11-year-old fan recently got the chance to join a Truro, N.S., hockey team on the ice at the Rath Eastlink Community Centre, but not in the way you might expect. Linus Mulherin tells us what happened.

items.title: Peter Nygard's appeal of sexual assault convictions being heard Monday in Toronto
items.summary_auto: The Ontario Court of Appeal is set to hear Peter Nygard's appeal of his sexual assault convictions and prison sentence today, with his lawyers arguing the Toronto trial judge made errors including admitting the testimony of an expert on trauma.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | Protest messages over 'Bezos Met Gala'
items.summary_auto: Activists are projecting protest messages on buildings in New York City and erecting ads against Amazon's Jeff Bezos and his wife, the co-chairs and lead sponsors of the Met Gala, the star-studded fundraiser known for its extravagant fashion and high-profile guests.

items.title: 'Everyone is scared all the time': Bondi Beach inquiry hears from Australia's Jewish community
items.summary_auto: A wide-ranging Australian inquiry examining antisemitism in the country after a massacre at a Hanukkah celebration heard Monday from that escalating hatred has left Jewish people in Australia fearful and vulnerable.

items.title: Quadriplegic senior says changes to home care putting his health at risk
items.summary_auto: A 72-year-old man in Saanich, B.C., says his usual community health workers seem to have been replaced with workers who aren't trained to help facilitate his daily bowel routine.

items.title: Canada commits $270M to Ukraine as Carney addresses European summit in Armenia
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney says his government will contribute $270 million to help Ukraine secure critical military capabilities in its defence against Russia's full-scale invasion.

items.title: U.A.E. says Iran has resumed attacks as U.S. moves to reopen Strait of Hormuz
items.summary_auto: The United Arab Emirates said Monday it came under attack by Iran for the first time since a fragile ceasefire took hold in early April. The attacks appeared to be in response to U.S. President Donald Trump's latest efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global energy.

items.title: Reeling from crisis, P.E.I.'s oyster industry hopes disease-resistant seed from U.S. helps
items.summary_auto: Developed by Rutgers University in New Jersey, the seed is hoped to be resistant to MSX and dermo, the two diseases that have ravaged the province’s oyster industry.

items.title: Dog walker bitten on the job can't sue because she 'owned' the pet at the time, Ontario's highest court rules
items.summary_auto: Ontario's highest court has ruled a part-time dog walker who was bitten on the job cannot sue her clients for damages because, under provincial law, she's considered to have been the "owner" of the animal at the time.

items.title: Her sexual allegations against a sergeant were finally being investigated. Did a jealous officer trigger it?
items.summary_auto: An investigation into an Ottawa police sergeant who died in March is raising questions about what information the force had when sexual misconduct allegations made by college students surfaced four years ago and resurfaced three years ago — and about why the criminal investigation only began this year.

items.title: FIFA World Cup: Everything (and more) you need to know about the 16 stadiums
items.summary_auto: We take a closer look at the 16 World Cup stadiums for this year's tournament.

items.title: This mother says Air Canada ignored refund plea for months after her child's death
items.summary_auto: A Nova Scotia mother says following her 10-year-old daughter’s death, Air Canada failed to refund one of the flights for a trip she'd planned for them, leaving her to chase down answers for months during an already devastating time. Experts say the experience highlights a deeper problem — one where customers are left without clear answers or accountability during their most vulnerable moments.

items.title: Widow of Windsor officer murdered 20 years ago says Ont. support group is a ‘lifeline’
items.summary_auto: Twenty years ago, Shelley Atkinson says she experienced “the worst day of my life” after her husband Const. John Atkinson was shot and killed while working as a police officer in Windsor. The Survivors Of Law Enforcement (SOLE) group is made up of 25 widows across Ontario who have lost a spouse in the line of duty.

items.title: Rudy Giuliani is hospitalized 'in critical but stable condition,' spokesperson says
items.summary_auto: Rudolph Giuliani, the ex-mayor of New York City and a former adviser to U.S. President Donald Trump, is in critical but stable condition at a hospital, his spokesperson said Sunday.

items.title: Habs beat out Lightning in Game 7, move on to face Buffalo Sabres
items.summary_auto: This is the first time the Canadiens are moving on to the second round since 2021. They'll now face the Buffalo Sabres. The two teams will kick off their series on Wednesday.

items.title: Hundreds of youths descend on Vancouver's Scientology building as part of speedrunning trend
items.summary_auto: The viral trend, dubbed "Scientology speedrunning," took off in early April, with users on social media posting videos of themselves entering — or in Saturday’s case trying to enter — properties owned or inhabited in some way by the Church of Scientology.
